Combine: %s %s 17.6 µl target DNA (880 ng) (DCW1 entry vector) 8.8 µl Tn7 donor DNA (220 ng) (pRZ101) 11 µl 20mM ATP 11 µl 20mM DTT 56.1 µl ddH2O This protocol delineates the steps to perform a small (20 µl) “‐ enzyme” negative control reaction and a large (80 µl) “+ enzyme” experimental mutagenesis reaction in parallel. The enzymes and buffers are mixed together in a master mix (5.5‐reaction sized, to account for pipetting error) and later split into appropriate reaction sizes.
Aliquot reaction mix into two PCR tubes. Dispense 76μl into the “+ enzyme” reaction tube, and 19μl into the “‐ enzyme” reaction tube.

Make the enzyme mixture. Combine: %s %s 7.49 μl TnsA 3.31 μl Storage buffer A 2.00 μl TnsB 6 μl Storage buffer B 8 μl TnsCA255V 8 μl Storage buffer C 5.2 μl 50% glycerol Mix by flicking tube gently. Keep on ice while setting up transposition reaction. Note 1: The ratio of TnsA, TnsB, and TnsC A255V in this mixture was determined empirically. You may need to optimize the relative amount of each enzyme using your purified enzyme stocks. Note 2: You may refreeze the enzyme mixture at ‐80C and rethaw twice, but will have decreased transposition efficiency with each thaw. We do not recommend refreezeing the stock solution of the individual Tns enzymes, so take care to store these in small aliquots so as to avoid wasting purified enzyme.
Make the buffer mixture for the “‐ enzyme” control. Combine: %s %s 10.8 μl Storage buffer A 8.0 μl Storage buffer B 16.0 μl Storage buffer C 5.2 μl 50% glycerol
Add 4 μl enzyme mix to the “+ enzyme” tube. Flick tube to mix
Add 1 μl of the buffer mixture to the “‐ enzyme” tube. Flick tube to mix.
Incubate both tubes at 37°C for 10 minutes on a PCR heat block. 00:10:00
Add 300mM MgOAc to the tubes: For “+ enzyme” reactions, add 4.2 μl 300mM MgOAc For “‐ enzyme” reaction, add 1.05 μl 300mM MgOac
Incubate at 37°C for 1 hour on a PCR block 01:00:00
Incubate at 75°C for 5 minutes on a PCR block to heat‐kill the enzymes. 00:05:00
Transfer “+ enzyme” and “‐ enzyme” reactions to 15ml microfuge tubes Bring the volume of each up to 100 μl.
Add 100 μl phenol:chloroform:IAA. Vortex to mix. undetermined phenol:chloroform:IAA Amresco Catalog #0883‐100ml
Spin 5 minutes, 4°C, 13500 rpm in a microfuge. 00:05:00
Remove and discard organic (bottom) layer.
Add 100 μl chloroform. Vortex to mix.
Spin 5 minutes, 4°C, 13500rpm in microfuge. 00:05:00
Transfer aqueous (top) later to a new 1.5ml microfuge tube.
Add 10 μl 3M NaOAc.
Add 220 μl ice‐cold 100% EtOH and chill at ‐20°C for 15 minutes. 00:15:00
Spin 15 minutes, 4°C, 13500 rpm in microfuge. Discard supernatant. 00:15:00
Wash DNA pellet with 500 μl ice‐cold 70% EtOH.
Spin 5 minutes, 4°C, 13500 rpm in microfuge. Discard supernatant. Allow pellet to air dry. 00:05:00
Resuspend DNA pellets in desired volume of 10mM Tris pH 7.5. For “+ enzyme” reactions, use 8 μl Tris pH 7.5 For “‐ enzyme” reaction, use 4 μl Tris pH 7.5
Optional: Cleaned transposition reactions may be stored at ‐20°C prior to transformation.

Combine 2 μl DNA with 20 μl MegaX cells in a chilled electroporation cuvette. undetermined Transform cleaned transposition reaction DNA into Invitrogen MegaX DH10B T1R electrocompetent E. coli cells. One set of steps is described for the “+ enzyme” reactions, another for “‐ enzyme” reactions. They can be performed in parallel, but are separated here for clarity. MegaX DH10B T1R Electrocomptent Cells Life Technologies Catalog #C6400‐03
Electroporate at 2.0 kV, 200Ω, 25μF, according to protocol.
Add 1ml Oxoid Isosensitest media to the cuvette and pipet up and down to resuspend.
Transfer 900 μl cells to a 1.5ml microfuge tube.
Recover at 37°C for 1 hour. 01:00:00
Plate 9 μl (1% of total cells) onto three types of selective media – LB+Kan, Iso + Tmp, and Iso+Tmp+Kan .
Grow plates overnight at 37°C. 18:00:00
Combine 4 μl DNA with 40 μl MegaX cells in one chilled electroporation cuvette. undetermined MegaX DH10B T1R Electrocomptent Cells Life Technologies Catalog #C6400‐03
Electroporate at 2.0 kV, 200Ω, 25μF, according to protocol.
Add 1ml Oxoid Isosensitest media to the cuvette and pipet up and down to resuspend.
Transfer 900 μl cells to a 500ml flask containing 150 mls Isosensistest media.
Recover at 37°C for 1 hour. 01:00:00
Plate 150 μl(0.1% of total cells) onto three types of selective media – LB+Kan, Iso + Tmp, and Iso+Tmp+Kan
Add 150 μl of Kan (30mg/ml stock) and 300μl Tmp (5mg/ml stock) to the flask.
Grow plates and culture overnight at 37°C. 18:00:00
The following morning, count the colonies growing on each plate. Calculate the number of independent transformants in your “+ enzyme” pools.
Make a glycerol frozen stock of the “+ enzyme” overnight culture. Store at ‐80°C
Pellet the remainder of the “+ enzyme” overnight culture in a 250ml conical tube. Spin 15 minutes at 3000 rpm. Pellet can be stored at ‐20°C, or continue to step 43 directly. 00:15:00

Purify the DNA from the “+ enzyme” culture cells using a Qiagen HiSpeed Midiprep Kit. Elute in 1ml 10mM Tris. This is the mutagenized DCW1*FLAG entry vector pool (ep). Note: You can monitor the plasmid population content by restriction enzyme digestion of the DNA pool. We have observed about 50% of the plasmid pool will be unmutagenized target vector. We suspect that the MegaX may take up multiple plasmids during transformation and do not segregate them properly Qiagen Hi‐Speed MidiPrep kit Qiagen Catalog #12643

Identify a restriction enzyme that cuts only in the backbone of the mutagenized DCW1*FLAG pool. We chose ApaLI, which cuts in the origin of replication. Linearizing the mutagenized entry pool increases the efficiency of the Gateway LR reaction. Additionally, it minimizes the amount of Kan‐marked plasmid that is present in the subsequent pools. ApaLI - 2,500 units New England Biolabs Catalog #R0507S
Digest 1μg of DNA from the DCW1*FLAG mutagenized entry pool in a 20μl reaction. Include a negative control – use 100ng of DNA in a 20μl reaction.
Digest 37°C for 1 hour. 01:00:00

Run the entire negative control reaction, and 2μl of the ApaLI digestion, on a gel to verify the backbone is fully digested. If digestion was successful, proceed. undetermined ApaLI - 2,500 units New England Biolabs Catalog #R0507S
Add 82μl ddH2O to the remaining ApaLI‐digested DNA, to bring it to a final volume of 100μl.
Add 100μl phenol:chloroform:IAA. Vortex to mix. undetermined phenol:chloroform:IAA Amresco Catalog #0883‐100ml
Spin 5 minutes, 4ºC, 13500 rpm in microfuge. Remove and discard organic (bottom) layer. 00:05:00
Add 100 μl chloroform. Vortex to mix.
Spin 5 minutes, 4°C, 13500 rpm in microfuge. Transfer aqueous (top) later to a new 1.5ml microfuge tube. 00:05:00
Add 10 μl 3M NaOAc.
Add 220μl ice‐cold 100% EtOH and chill at ‐20°C for 15 minutes. 00:15:00
Spin 15 minutes, 4°C, 13500 rpm in microfuge. Discard supernatant. 00:15:00
Wash DNA pellet with 500μl ice‐cold 70% EtOH.
Spin 5 minutes, 4°C, 13500 rpm in microfuge. Discard supernatant. Allow pellet to air dry. 00:05:00
Resuspend DNA in 20μl 10mM Tris. DNA will be 45ng/μl.

Make a 2.5x reaction mixture DNA, which will be split into +LR and –LR reactions. Combine: 371.25ng (8.25μl) mutagenized, linearized entry vector 371.25ng pRZ159 (DCW1 destination vector) Bring to 20μl total volume with TE 1 reaction volume uses 150ng destination vector and 150ng (mutagenized, linearized) entry vector

For the “+LR” experimental reaction, combine 16μl of the DNA mixture from step 59 and 4μl Invitrogen LR Clonase II in a PCR tube. For the “‐LR” control reaction, combine 4μl DNA mixture from step 59 and 1μl TE in a PCR tube. Incubate both reactions overnight at 25°C on the PCR block. 18:00:00
Stop the LR reaction by adding proteinase K to the reactions: Add 2μl proteinase K to the “+LR” experimental reaction; Add 0.5μl proteinase K to the “‐LR” negative control.
Incubate at 37°C for 10 minutes on the PCR block. 00:10:00

Clean reactions: Transfer reactions to 1.5ml microfuge tubes. Perform phenol/chloroform extraction and EtOH precipitation as described before (“Gateway LR mobilization to create expression pools: Clean linearized DNA”)
Resuspend “+LR” experimental DNA in 6μl TE (final [DNA] = 50ng/ul).
Resuspend “‐LR” negative control DNA in 4μl TE (final [DNA] = 18.75ng/ul).

Combine 2μl DNA with 20μl MegaX cells in a chilled electroporation cuvette. undetermined Transform cleaned LR reaction DNA into Invitrogen MegaX DH10B T1R electrocompetent E. coli cells. One set of steps is described for the “+ enzyme” reactions, another for “‐ enzyme” reactions. They can be performed in parallel, but are separated here for clarity. Note that the Car and Tmp selections (intended to select only those expression plasmids with a DCW1 orf and a Tn insertion) are performed in sequential rounds of transformation. We found simultaneous Car and Tmp drug selection seemed to pressure cells into maintaining both an unmutagenized DCW1 expression vector and a mutagenized entry vector. MegaX DH10B T1R Electrocomptent Cells Life Technologies Catalog #C6400‐03
Electroporate at 2.0 kV, 200Ω, 25μF, according to protocol.
Add 1ml Recovery media (provided with MegaX cells) to the cuvette and pipet up and down to resuspend.

Purify the DNA from the “+ enzyme” culture cells using a Qiagen HiSpeed Midiprep Kit. Elute in 1ml 10mM Tris. This is the expression pool, selected for Car R (“xpC”). Qiagen Hi‐Speed MidiPrep kit Qiagen Catalog #12643

Digest 500ng of xpT pool with FseI ; use 100ng of xpT pool for a “no enzyme” control. %s %s No enzyme Fsel 100 ng 500 ng xpT pool DNA 1 μl 1 μl 10x NEB buffer #4 1 μl 1μl 10x BSA 0 μl 0.5 μl FseI To 10μl total To 10μl total dH2O Restriction digestions to remove Tn7 DNA from plasmids At this point, the pools contain expression plasmids with mutagenized target ORF. This is based on the ability to grown in MegaX cells – confirming the expression backbone instead of the entry or destination vectors, and the selection for both Car R and Tmp R ‐ confirming the expression backbone (Car R ) and the presence of Tn7 (Tmp R ). At this time, the Tn7 ends and Tmp R gene are no longer needed, and are removed from the plasmids using a series of restriction digests. FseI - 100 units New England Biolabs Catalog #R0588S
Incubate restriction digests and control reactions at 37°C for 1hr. 01:00:00
Heat inactivate FseI by incubating reactions at 65°C for 20 minutes. 00:20:00 FseI - 100 units New England Biolabs Catalog #R0588S

Run all of the “no enzyme” control and 2μl (100ng of DNA) from FseI digest on an agarose gel to confirm restriction digest was successful. The uncut control should contain supercoiled plasmid, and the FseI digest should have a high MW band of linearized plasmid DNA; the Tn7L fragment is 211bp long, though this fragment is sometimes not visible. If successful, continue with protocol. FseI - 100 units New England Biolabs Catalog #R0588S
Add 12 μl of dH2O to remaining FseI‐digested xpT material to bring final volume to 20 μl.

Digest 1 μg of xpT‐L pool with PmeI; use 100ng of xpT‐L pool for a “no enzyme” control. %s %s No enzyme PmeI 100 ng 1000 ng xpT pool DNA 1 μl 1 μl 10x NEB buffer #4 1 μl 1 μl 10x BSA 0 μl 0.5 μl PmeI To 10μl total To 10μl total dH2O PmeI - 500 units New England Biolabs Catalog #R0560S
Incubate restriction digests and control reactions at 37°C for 1hr. 01:00:00

Run all of the “no enzyme” control and 1μl (100ng of DNA) from PmeI digest on an agarose gel to confirm restriction digest was successful. The uncut control should contain supercoiled plasmid. The PmeI digest should have a high MW band of plasmid backbone DNA, and the released Tn7R end should be visible at 863bp. If successful, continue with protocol.
Add ddH2O to remaining PmeI‐digested xpT‐L material to bring final volume to 100μl. We found that residual PmeI may interfere with the success of the ligation reaction.
Add 100μl phenol:chloroform:IAA. Vortex to mix. undetermined phenol:chloroform:IAA Amresco Catalog #0883‐100ml
Spin 5 minutes, 4°C, 13500 rpm in microfuge. Remove and discard organic (bottom) layer. 00:05:00
Add 100 μl chloroform. Vortex to mix.
Spin 5 minutes, 4°C, 13500 rpm in microfuge. 00:05:00
Transfer aqueous (top) later to a new 1.5ml microfuge tube.
Add 10 μl 3M NaOAc.
Add 220μl ice‐cold 100% EtOH and chill at ‐20°C for 15 minutes. 00:15:00
Spin 15 minutes, 4°C, 13500 rpm in microfuge. Discard supernatant. 00:15:00
Wash DNA pellet with 500μl ice‐cold 70% EtOH.
Spin 5 minutes, 4°C, 13500 rpm in microfuge. Discard supernatant. Allow pellet to air dry. 00:05:00
Resuspend DNA pellet (may not be visible) in 20μl of 10mM Tris pH 7.5.

Ligate at 16°C, 16 hours. 16:00:00

Combine 1μl of DNA (~ 112ng/μl) with 20μl MegaX cells in a chilled electroporation cuvette. undetermined Follow steps 153-156 for both “‐ control” and “+ ligase” reactions: MegaX DH10B T1R Electrocomptent Cells Life Technologies Catalog #C6400‐03
